data.title: What's Going On With Ondas Stock Today?
data.description:
Ondas Inc. (NASDAQ:ONDS) shares are up on Friday following a significant new contract announcement.

The company secured a “multi-million dollar” order for its Iron Drone Raider system from a major European customer in a NATO country, which is expected to enhance its position in the civil infrastructure protection market.

The new contract highlights the growing demand for operationally ready counter-UAS systems in civil environments, particularly as hostile drone activity increases.

“The rise in hostile drone activity has reshaped security requirements for airports and urban critical infrastructure,” said Eric Brock, Chairman and CEO of Ondas. “These regulated environments require counter-UAS systems that operate safely without disrupting communications or creating collateral risk”.

This order follows a previous $16.8 million contract for the deployment of the Iron Drone Raider at two major European international airports.

Yesterday, Ondas said its smart demining subsidiary won a tender valued at over $30 million for a multi-year land-clearance program along the Israel-Syria border. The project represents one of the largest demining efforts ever undertaken in Israel.

The program will be executed based on defined milestones, with an initial execution period of up to three years and options for extensions and scope expansion. The project covers approximately 741 acres in a historically contaminated and strategically sensitive region.

Technical Analysis

Currently, Ondas Inc. is trading 13.2% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 5.9% above its 100-day SMA, indicating some short-term weakness but longer-term strength. Shares have increased 400% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 41.25, which is considered neutral territory, while MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $10.00
  • Key Support: $8.50

Ondas’s performance today contrasts sharply with the sector’s recent trend, as the Communication Services sector has declined by 1.59% over the past 30 days.

The recent contract win underscores the firm’s strategic focus on expanding its footprint in the autonomous systems market, particularly in civil infrastructure protection. As demand for advanced security solutions grows, the company is well-positioned to capitalize on these opportunities.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Ondas is slated to provide its next financial update on Mar. 11, 2026.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 4 cents (Up from Loss of 15 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $27.30 million (Up from $4.13 million YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price forecast of $13.06. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Stifel: Buy (Raises Target to $18.00) (Jan. 21)
  • Lake Street: Buy (Raises Target to $19.00) (Jan. 20)
  • Oppenheimer: Outperform (Raises Target to $16.00) (Jan. 20)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Ondas, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 99.15) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Ondas’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals strong momentum, indicating that the stock is currently outperforming the market. Traders should monitor for continued strength as the company expands its market presence.

ONDS Price Action: Ondas shares were up 5.96% at $9.51 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by T. Schneider via Shutterstock

data.title: 'A Big, Bipartisan Group Working Hard' On Crypto Bill As Paul Atkins Warns On Conflicts Of Interest
data.description:
According to Sen. Mark Warner (D-VI), a "big, bipartisan group working hard" to advance the Senate's crypto market structure bill, even as talks face mounting obstacles.

Future-Proof Policy Requires Congress              

During a Senate Banking Committee hearing, SEC Chairman Paul Atkins cautioned that while the agency can issue rules under his "Project Crypto" agenda, any clarity created solely through SEC action could be reversed by a future administration.

Atkins stressed that durable, "future-proof" crypto regulation requires legislation passed by Congress, not just agency rulemaking, CoinDesk reported on Friday.

The proposed Digital Asset Market Clarity Act continues to face sticking points. Disputes remain between crypto firms and banks over stablecoin rewards, while some Democrats have raised concerns about regulatory staffing levels and potential conflicts of interest. Lawmakers are also divided over how decentralized finance platforms should address illicit finance risks.

Warner, a leading Democratic negotiator, emphasized the need to prevent bad actors from exploiting DeFi while avoiding overly broad enforcement loopholes, signaling that additional safeguards are required before securing broader Democratic backing.

Industry Pressure Builds

A version of the Clarity Act has passed the House and cleared the Senate Agriculture Committee, but final Senate approval will require bipartisan support.

Senate Banking Committee Chairman Tim Scott (R-SC) has expressed optimism about reaching a deal.

However, industry leaders such as Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) CEO Brian Armstrong have warned they could withdraw support if the final framework proves unfavorable.

The White House has urged negotiators to strike a compromise, underscoring that SEC-led regulatory efforts remain vulnerable without clear congressional authorization.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: What's Going On With Lucid Stock Friday?
data.description:
Lucid Group Inc. (NASDAQ:LCID) stock rose on Friday. This movement aligns with the Utilities sector’s 1.44% gain, suggesting a favorable environment for stocks like Lucid.

The Newark, California-based electric vehicle manufacturer has seen its short interest decrease during the most recent reporting period, falling from 45.11 million to 43.37 million shares.

About 51.78% of the company’s publicly available shares are currently held short. With a recent average daily volume of 9.48 million shares, it would take short sellers 4.58 days to cover their positions. This high short interest ratio often serves as a key indicator for retail traders looking for potential “short squeeze” opportunities.

Record-Breaking Winter Performance

Investor Day Set For March 12

Investors are turning their attention to the company’s Investor Day on March 12, where management will outline its strategic roadmap. Leadership is expected to highlight the upcoming midsize vehicle program, progress in software development and steps toward stronger financial performance. The event will also offer a first look at Lucid’s next-generation vehicle architecture, a key element in its plan to scale production volumes.

Technical Analysis

Lucid Group is currently trading 1.4% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 8.8% below its 50-day SMA, indicating a short-term bearish sentiment. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 68.16%, and they are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, reflecting ongoing challenges in the market.

The RSI is at 42.10, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting that the stock is not yet in oversold or overbought conditions. Meanwhile, MACD is above its signal line, indicating bullish momentum, which could suggest potential for upward movement if market conditions remain favorable.

The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while there is some bullish pressure, the stock is not yet showing strong upward trends.

  • Key Resistance: $12.00
  • Key Support: $9.50

LCID Price Action: Lucid Group shares were up 6.45% at $10.56 at the time of publication on Friday. The stock is near its 52-week low of $9.50, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: What's Going On With Intensity Therapeutics Stock Friday?
data.description:
Intensity Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ:INTS) shares are tumbling on Friday following the announcement of a 1-for-25 reverse stock split to increase the per-share trading price and comply with Nasdaq listing requirements.

The stock’s decline comes as broader markets are experiencing gains, with the S&P 500 up 0.24% and the Nasdaq rising 0.29%.

The reverse stock split, approved by shareholders in October 2025, is set to take effect at 4:01 p.m. Eastern Time on February 18, 2026, with trading on a post-split adjusted basis beginning on February 19, 2026. This move will reduce the number of outstanding shares from approximately 63.3 million to about 2.5 million, which is expected to help the company regain compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement.

In addition to the split, Intensity Therapeutics is focused on its lead product candidate, INT230-6, which is designed for direct intratumoral injection.

The company aims to leverage its proprietary DfuseRx technology to enhance the effectiveness of cancer therapies.

The broader market is experiencing positive momentum, with the Technology sector gaining 1.47% today. Despite this, Intensity Therapeutics’ stock is down, indicating that company-specific factors are influencing its performance.

Technical Analysis

The stock is currently trading 30.8% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 36.1% below its 50-day SMA, indicating significant weakness in its short-term trend.

Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 87.94%, and they are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, suggesting ongoing challenges for the company.

The RSI is at 46.41, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is above its signal line, indicating a potential bullish signal. The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum for the stock.

  • Key Resistance: 50 cents
  • Key Support: 20 cents

Earnings Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the March 12, 2026, earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 5 cents (Up from Loss of 22 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $0.00 M

INTS Price Action: Intensity Therapeutics shares were down 26.70% at $0.27 at the time of publication on Friday. The stock is near its 52-week low of $0.18, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by jittawit21 via Shutterstock

data.title: Her Boyfriend Won't Get A Job Because 'It Wouldn't Benefit Him.' She's Covering The Bills And Wondering, 'Am I Being Financially Abused?'
data.description:
For over two years, Madison has been the sole provider for her household while her partner refuses to work. His reason? He told her getting a job “wouldn't benefit him” because she would just misuse the money.

Unemployed Partner, Full-Time Excuses

This situation came to light during a call to “The Ramsey Show,” where she asked the hosts a question that's been weighing on her: “Am I being financially abused?” The couple had remodeled and launched an Airbnb together, which her boyfriend pointed to as his contribution. But as Madison explained, “It's paying the bills but it's not turning a profit.”

Don't Miss:

When she asked whether her situation qualified as abuse, co-host Ken Coleman was straightforward. “That statement you gave [co-host] George [Kamel] and I,” he said, “where he looked at you with a straight face… and said a job would not benefit him because you would misuse the money, is about as manipulative as I’ve ever seen.”

“This guy is an absolutely broken human being,” he continued. “And that's not an insult. That's an actual diagnosis.”

Kamel emphasized that while they couldn't legally classify the situation as abuse, what Madison described was serious manipulation and a relationship with no financial partnership or responsibility.

Trending: Designed for investors with strong market convictions, REX Shares builds ETFs for income, leverage, and tactical positioning — explore the lineup.

A Wake-Up Call in Disguise

Madison admitted she stays because of their children but said the situation has become emotionally exhausting. Coleman challenged her to imagine if her friend were in the same situation. “What would your advice to her be?” he asked. Madison responded that she'd urge her friend to seek counseling and leave if things didn't improve. “The advice you gave your friend is the advice for yourself,” Coleman said. “You know what to do, so go do it.”

The hosts emphasized that her boyfriend has no right to her money, especially since they aren't married. Any joint finances should be separated immediately.

If you're facing a complex money situation like Madison's and don't know where to begin, WiserAdvisor's free matching tool can help. It connects people with a household income of $100,000 or more to vetted financial advisors for free consultations—with zero obligation to hire anyone.

At the end of the call, Coleman reminded Madison that this isn’t just about her anymore. “This is about those kiddos,” he said. “Take care of you, so that you can take care of them.”

Image: Shutterstock
